title: Liabilities Project (Los Angeles City Attorney's Office)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Copies of all reports, lists, databases, or individual documents created since 1/1/16 detail lawsuits brought against the city's police department. This should include any documentation mechanism your department uses to track lawsuits. It should also include any documents your department submits to local, state, or federal agencies listing the number and status of lawsuits brought against the police department. This request should include documents compiled quarterly, annually, or in other increments and documents or databases that contain responsive information over a longer period. 

- The records should include, if it exists, the following information: 
Settlement amounts
Case category (civil rights, collisions, etc) 
Case type/description (harassment, crowd control, in-custody death, etc)
Involved officers
Case numbers
Total expenses (this should include the legal costs to the city, including outsite litigation costs.) 

- Any corrective action reports that describe the incident that caused the lawsuit and the corrective actions taken to minimize risk, prevent recurrence, and hold the department and the involved officers accountable. The reports are often sent to respective bodies to approve settlements (city council, board of supervisors, controller, or risk management office) or created by another office within the city or county. 
- Copies of settlement agreements 

Only include settlements above $75,000 and lawsuits related to civil rights violations, collisions, professional negligence, and labor.

Search January 1, 2016 to today's date.

When possible, produce documents in spreadsheet format (excel, csv). California public records law requires the custodian of records to assist in finding records.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 days.

Sincerely,

J.

title: Charlie Kirk Assassination - Indiana Attorney General (Second Request)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Indiana Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any submission to the "Education Transparency Form" on or after September 1, 2025. The form is located at https://in.accessgov.com/attorneygeneral/Forms/Page/eyesoneducation/education-transparency-form.

2. Any public records request submitted by or on behalf of the Office of the Attorney General to a public university or college since January 1, 2025.

3. Any text messages sent or received on or after September 10, 2025, by Todd Rokita, Lori Ann Tores, Clinton Bohm, Michael R. Ward, or Joshua DeFonce containing any of the following terms: "Kirk", "Ball State", "Felicia Gray", "Swierc", "Franklin College", "Colburn", "Henery", "Bloomington", "Maurer", "Dau-Schmidt", "Sanders", "Munden", "Hale", "Kraig", "Blackwelder".

Please note that this request seeks public records wherever they may be found, including on devices or networks owned or operated by private parties. For example, if an employee uses a personal cell phone or social media account to conduct public business, records created, maintained, or held on the phone or account are public records subject to this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Adam Steinbaugh

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: 1321 Piney Forest Road FCV Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide information regarding any open Fire Code Violations for the following property: 1321 Piney Forest Road (Woodside Village)

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Joshua O'fair.
